protocol_notes: Toyota uses mode 0x21 (KWP2000 readDataByLocalIdentifier) on most pre-2015 engines. Newer platforms (2015+) use mode 0x22 (UDS). In Torque Pro notation, '221074' means mode=0x22, PID=0x1074. Engine ECU typically at CAN header 0x700 (request) / 0x7E8 (response). Some modules at different addresses.
sources: gr-zoo.com/threads/pids-reverse-engineered-revisited — GR Yaris PIDs, formulas verified by physically unplugging sensors
mr2oc.com/threads/data-logging-the-2gr-fe-ecu — RAV4 2GR-FE CAN bus decode, cross-verified with TechStream
rav4world.com transmission temp threads — formula confirmed against TechStream (158.5F vs 160.5F delta)
fjcruiserforums.com Toyota specific PIDs — knock correction/feedback
confidence_notes: Toyota PIDs are more fragmented than VAG. HIGH = formula verified against TechStream or by physical sensor manipulation. MEDIUM = community confirmed working. LOW = single source or inferred.
important_note: Toyota mode 0x21 PIDs use KWP2000 service 0x21 (not UDS 0x22). Hudson currently only implements 0x22. A separate query_kwp_local() function is needed for full Toyota support on pre-2015 vehicles. Mode 0x22 PIDs (identifiers >= 0x1000) work with the existing UDS path.

mode_21_identifiers.note: These require KWP2000 service 0x21, NOT UDS 0x22. Hudson TODO: implement query_kwp_local()

0x21D9.label: Transmission fluid temperature (torque converter)
0x21D9.notes: Verified against TechStream on RAV4 2GR-FE. Response is multi-frame (8 bytes). A/B are bytes 1/2, E/F are bytes 5/6 (second sensor, may be 0x0000 if not fitted). Source: MR2OC RAV4 CAN decode.

variables.pan_temp: E/F (bytes 5-6, may not be present on all variants)

0x21B2.label: Knock correction / feedback values
0x21B2.notes: Knock Correction Learn Value and Knock Feedback Value. Verified visible in TechStream. Source: FJCruiser forums.

mode_22_identifiers.note: These use UDS service 0x22 and work with Hudson's existing query_uds(). Used on newer Toyota platforms (approx 2015+).

0x1074.label: Engine oil pressure
0x1074.notes: Verified on GR Yaris by community. Source: gr-zoo.com PIDs thread.

0x1638.label: Transmission oil temperature
0x1638.notes: Verified on GR Yaris, reading ~50°C at operating temp, ~60°C after spirited driving. Source: gr-zoo.com PIDs thread.

0x1021.label: Battery / alternator voltage
0x1021.notes: B+ alternator voltage. Source: gr-zoo.com GR Yaris PIDs, header 7C0 (combination meter ECU).

0x1059.label: Accelerator pedal position
0x1059.notes: Source: gr-zoo.com GR Yaris reverse engineering thread.

multi_value_pids.note: Toyota often packs many values into a single PID response. 0x2101 on Prius contains 26 values including RPM, coolant temp, MAF, throttle, speed, battery voltage all in one response.

0x2101.label: Combined engine data block (Prius/hybrid)
0x2101.notes: Hybrid-specific combined data block. Not applicable to conventional RAV4. Source: Torque Pro Prius predefined PIDs.

health_check_notes.transmission_temp: Normal operating: 50-90°C. High load: up to 120°C acceptable. Above 150°C indicates potential issue.
health_check_notes.oil_pressure: Idle warm: ~1.5-2.5 bar typical. Under load: 3.5-5 bar typical.
